solutions.solution: The statement clearly says that "Beer is good for health". Hence, I is not stated in the statement. But merely that it is a mere restatement. Remember that an assumption is something assumed and not something explicitly stated. III may or may not be true. The statement mentions obesity but does not say that it is the only disadvantage of Beer.
The question of the daughter's agreement has not been touched. Maybe a Will man's his daughter even without her agreement. It is obvious to us statement. III is not clear.

I is obvious by the way the Principal says that those students shall be punished. It implies that he can punish them. III is obvious. II may not be correct. It should have been: a punishment may follow warnings only if there is no effect of those warnings.
The passenger is merely uttering complaint; obviously, he has no authority to ban them. II is obvious. III may or may not be assumed. The passenger. He may think that his colleague may disagree.

I is not an assumption; it is merely a restatement. II is implicit. If the lawyer cites autopsy to prove that the cause of death was torture, he must be assuming that autopsy can help in determining the cause of death. III is not implicit; it is not told that the lawyer performed the autopsy himself.

I is implicit: the use of the word 'probably' indicates that the cause of the collapse is probably the rumour of the FM's resignation but it is not definitely known. It is implicit: if the rumour of the FM's resignation can make the important man quit, then the rumour must be important to the brokers. III is not implicit: the rumours may or may not be baseless.

Since the author describes the thoughts of Godse, he must be assuming that he knows the character of Godse well. Since Godse intended to kill anybody, we can say that Godse killed Rashtra and since he killed Gandhi for these reasons, it is obvious that he thought Gandhi to be an obstruction to his idea. III is not implicit; it is not the meaning of the given sentence. He might have had his reasons but these reasons themselves could be very irrational.

I is obviously implicit that disagreement exists and that it can be resolved through peaceful means. Implies that disagreement can be resolved through peaceful means. For the same reasons, II is also implicit.

Sampras does not make any comment about how a tennis court should be. He only observes what it was. Hence, neither I nor II is implicit. III is implicit: the court played fast and, in this situation, Yzaga was at a disadvantage. It implies that Yzaga is a better player at slower courts.
All are implicit. Since she gives emphasis on the fact that he has not changed, it means that some people do change after success; and hence

I is implicit. Since the star quotes Bazaigor while saying that success has not changed him it implies that Bazaigor is a successful film. Hence, I is implicit. Finally, the statement "Why should it?" implies III.

The first line states that it may result against shock waves. The second line promises to issue against dangerous. Hence, it is implicit that earthquakes and shock waves are almost synonymous. Hence I is implicit. It is implicit because there is no hint in the second sentence. Because of this word the meaning of this sentence becomes: there are many companies which offer life insurance against earthquakes but Oriental Insurance offers the most comprehensive insurance. III is not implicit. Although it is a well-known fact that earthquakes are a natural phenomenon, the given statement never mentions nature.

That the people were hesitant to follow non-violence is implied by the first sentence. It, in turn, implies that they were planning to use violence; hence I is implicit. II is not implicit in the second sentence. III is also implicit: the Sikhs wanted to say that Gandhi could easily preach non-violence because his own folk were not affected by violence. This implies III.

Gandhi accepts that tragedy occurred for the Sikhs and yet he advocates non-violence. It means that he thought non-violence to be the best method. This implies I. By admitting that the women and his sons of Sikhs were like his own, Gandhi shares their sorrow and hence II is implicit. Also, Gandhi was persuading the Sikhs because he thought that his persuasion may work; that his rational approach may transform the Sikhs. Hence III is also implicit.

The use of the phrase "all the way" implies that Maya Nagar is far off. There is no hint, however, that the man making the statement got his ticket. Similarly, it cannot be declared that the movie is a superhit. There are many films which flop despite running house-full for a first few days.

I may or may not be true. Since the lawyer does not contradict the statement that a short man was seen, it implies that he assumes it to be true. Hence, I is implicit. Also, the short man should not be Khushogi because the latter is five-feet-eight, which implies that III is implicit. It is just the opposite of truth. II is not implicit by the engineer's statement. He makes no hints at previous planning engineers, etc. III is obviously valid.

I is obviously implicit. Either II or III must be true and you may be tempted to choose 4 as your answer choice. But the question is about assumption. The doctor's statement does not assume either II or III. Although, any one of these two must be true but they are not assumptions. See "Some tips for Quick Answers" at the end of the lesson, before the Exercises.
